headline: Agent memory and local-first tooling lead the month while quantization and on-device inference break out across new model releases.
body: Agent memory and local-first tools top the rotation, anchored by Hermes, an open-source agent framework with persistent memory, skill generation, and local execution on Mac and Windows that now carries nearly 200K GitHub stars, plus a companion desktop tool that preserves Claude session context across projects. On the model side, quantization breaks out alongside Gemma 4, whose 2B model is reported to match last year's 27B dense model and ships for offline inference through the Google AI Edge Gallery. Enterprise multi-model platforms also advanced, with Microsoft making Copilot Cowork generally available and weighing DeepSeek as a lower-cost Azure option to route work across OpenAI, Claude, Cowork, and DeepSeek. Embeddings, encoder-decoder, and Rectified Flow round out the breaking techniques, while delegation chains, step reduction, and robot policy entered as new entrants.

cited_items.title: Microsoft And DeepSeek Are Launching New AI Together
Gemma 4 and the AI Edge Gallery: On-Device AI Gets an Upgrade
Hermes Agent Tutorial: How to Setup & Use Open Source AI Agent for Beginners
